WASHINGTON, d. c., May A.
In his coming letter to the New
York Federation of Labor on the
Moyer-Haywood case, promised
Sunday. it is understood the presi-
dent will make it clear his position
as to the "undesirability of these
men is not taken without investiga-
ton. 1t is stated at the depart
ment of justice that the president
over year ago, in answer to a
warning from Governor Gooding
that federal troops might be want
ed in that state. the president call
ed upon the department for an ex-
haustive investigation of the whole
case. including the acts against
Meyer and Haywood, of which the
laborites now complain.


BOISE, Idaho. May 1.-The com
ing trial of Haywood continues to
bring crowds Of strangers. Every
available room has been taken.
Fully 500 men will be here from
Colorado TO attend the trial. Attor-
neys for Haywood have filed a mo-
ton in the district court asking
that the prosecution furnish a bill
of particulars. 1t will be argued
Monday morning.


Meyer, Haywood and Pettibone
the courthouse lawn, but the mum
Der of guards has been increased
from 5 to 10.
